results.model: MOGT baseline_v1 shape + memory-efficient LM loss
MOGT baseline_v1 shape + memory-efficient LM loss

interpretation: This is a one-step systems probe, not a modeling-quality benchmark.
Before the memory-efficient LM loss, the MOGT 130M training path did not fit ctx=65536 on L4 24GB, even with loss_chunk_size reduced to 1.
After replacing the retained-logits chunked loss with a recompute-in-backward linear CE, MOGT fits one ctx=65536 training step.
With the same memory-efficient loss, the scratch Transformer baseline also fits ctx=65536.
The current evidence supports an LM-head memory fix and a possible step-time advantage for MOGT in a one-step systems probe; it does not prove modeling-quality superiority over Transformer.
next_actions: Rerun comparable 32k/64k step-time and peak-memory probes with the new LM loss for both MOGT and Transformer.
Run synthetic long-context tasks now that MOGT has a reliable 64k training/eval path.
Do not claim Transformer quality superiority until MOGT beats the scratch Transformer budget baseline or wins on a clearly scoped long-context task.
